codeReview.summary: Solid implementation of the catering inquiry form. The page structure follows conventions well: server component page.tsx with metadata, client component CateringContent.tsx with hero, image, and form sections. Form correctly uses manual Zod validation in onSubmit (safeParse + setError) without the redundant RHF validate prop — actually better than the Newsletter.tsx reference pattern. All required fields from features.json are present with proper validation. Design system compliance is strong: zero border-radius, proper transitions, section-padding/max-container utilities, uppercase labels. Two minor non-blocking issues found.

issues.description: The 'otherChecked' useState is not reset when the user clicks 'SUBMIT ANOTHER INQUIRY'. After a successful submission, reset() clears RHF form state but otherChecked remains true, causing the 'Other' checkbox to appear pre-checked and the 'Please specify' text field to be visible (but empty) when the form reappears. Should add setOtherChecked(false) alongside reset() in onSubmit, or in the setIsSuccess(false) handler.

Success state icon wrapper has conflicting display classes: 'inline-block' and 'flex' are both applied. 'flex' takes precedence so 'inline-block' is redundant. Should remove 'inline-block' for clarity.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md says 'Do NOT also add RHF validate prop (redundant dual validation)' and references Newsletter.tsx as the established pattern. However, Newsletter.tsx itself uses a handleValidation function passed to RHF's validate prop (line ~52: register('email', { validate: handleValidation })), contradicting the stated convention. The catering form correctly avoids this, but the reference pattern is inconsistent with the documented rule.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/components/sections/Newsletter.tsx:52 — uses { validate: handleValidation } in register call. AGENTS.md Forms section says 'See src/components/sections/Newsletter.tsx for the established pattern' while also saying 'Do NOT also add RHF validate prop'.

summary: The catering inquiry form implementation passes review. It correctly implements all required fields (name, email, phone, guests, budget, date, time, location, event type, dietary restrictions with Other option, cuisine preferences, service type, notes) with Zod validation, proper error display, and success state. The form follows the manual Zod validation pattern correctly (actually better than the Newsletter.tsx reference). Two non-blocking issues: (1) otherChecked state not reset on form re-display after success, (2) redundant inline-block class on success icon. One shared state observation about Newsletter.tsx contradicting the AGENTS.md convention it's cited as exemplifying.
